run_metadata.coverage_summary: Daily incremental run. Tier 1 swept via web search across Commonwealth (AusTender) and all state/territory portals (buy.nsw, Buying for Victoria, QTenders, Tenders WA, Tenders SA, Tenders Tasmania, NT QTOL, Tenders ACT). Direct fetches were broadly blocked this session (see gaps): AusTender, buy.nsw, tenders.vic.gov.au, VendorPanel, Sydney Water and Queensland Health all returned HTTP 403 to the fetch tool (target-side WAF), so opportunities were captured at headline level from search-result snippets and cross-checked across multiple queries. Four fresh, publicly-visible, buyer-originated opportunities captured this run, spanning NSW local government (Blacktown City Council), NSW state transport (Transport for NSW - two distinct opportunities) and NSW public health (Central Coast Local Health District). Tier 2/3 source discovery focus: two genuinely new buyer-originated sources recorded (the new Australian Government Supplier Portal opening for registration mid-2026, and Development Victoria's own Tenders & EOI channel). Tier 3 (SAP Ariba Discovery) confirmed as registration-gated with no publicly-visible AU opportunity extractable without login; recorded as a portal source only, not fabricated as tenders. Development Victoria's Commonwealth Games 2026 construction procurement surfaced but returned only stale/2023-dated timeline data and could not be confirmed as a current live opportunity, so it was deliberately NOT captured as a tender.
gaps: www.tenders.gov.au (AusTender) returned HTTP 403 to the fetch tool this run (egress/WAF block); the current ATM list and ATM detail pages could not be opened directly. Commonwealth coverage was headline-level via search only; no new Commonwealth opportunity was captured with confidence beyond items already tracked in prior runs.

buy.nsw.gov.au / tenders.nsw.gov.au returned HTTP 403 to the fetch tool; NSW opportunities (Blacktown City Council Christmas decorations RFT, TfNSW Bradfield Bus Interchange EOI, TfNSW Woolgoolga-to-Ballina koala monitoring RFT, Central Coast LHD EAP RFQ) were captured at headline level from search-result snippets and cross-checked, but full detail pages, documents, evaluation criteria and contacts could not be scraped - all flagged entries_pending_detail.

www.tenders.vic.gov.au, www.vendorpanel.com.au, www.sydneywater.com.au and www.health.qld.gov.au all returned HTTP 403 to the fetch tool; Victorian, council-platform, Sydney Water and Queensland Health tender detail pages could not be opened. No confirmed new opportunity captured from these this run beyond headline signals already known.

Development Victoria Commonwealth Games 2026 construction procurement (main works contractor EOI/RFP) surfaced in search but only returned stale timeline data (2023 dates) and could not be confirmed as a current live opportunity; deliberately not captured as a tender to avoid fabrication.

SAP Ariba Discovery / SAP Business Network Discovery (Tier 3, mandatory): opportunity list is registration-gated; no publicly-visible AU RFx could be captured without an authenticated supplier session. Recorded as a portal source only, not fabricated as a tender.

Tier 2 sampling only: most universities/TAFEs, the majority of local councils, and most health districts outside NSW were not exhaustively swept this run; coverage rotated to NSW transport/health/local-government. WA, SA, TAS, NT and ACT portals returned only known or headline-level items this run.
recommended_seed_additions: Australian Government Supplier Portal (https://suppliers.tenders.gov.au/): new Commonwealth supplier-registration database launching mid-2026, with supplier registration opening from July 2026; complements AusTender and is worth monitoring as agencies begin sourcing capability through it.

Development Victoria - Doing business with us / Tenders & EOI (https://www.development.vic.gov.au/about/tenders-and-eoi): Victorian state development GBE that runs EOIs/tenders (e.g. major precinct and Games-related works) directly on its own channel as well as via Buying for Victoria; sibling to other state development/works agencies.
Consider an authenticated SAP Ariba Discovery supplier account so future runs can read gated corporate RFx detail (carried from prior runs).

Consider a fetch path that is not WAF-blocked for the Tier-1 government portals (AusTender, buy.nsw, tenders.vic) so deliverables/documents can be extracted rather than left at headline level (carried from prior runs).

tenders.title: Provision, hire, installation, maintenance and removal of Christmas decorations - Blacktown Local Government Area
tenders.description: Blacktown City Council is inviting tenders from suitably qualified suppliers for the provision, hire, installation, maintenance and removal of Christmas decorations across the Blacktown Local Government Area.
tenders.deliverables_summary: Supply, hire, installation, ongoing maintenance and end-of-season removal of Christmas decorations across public places in the Blacktown LGA, delivering a safe, well-presented festive display for the Council's annual Christmas program.
key_deliverables: Installation across the Blacktown Local Government Area
Maintenance of decorations during the display period
Removal of decorations at end of season
requirements: Suitably qualified and experienced supplier (headline requirement stated in the notice; full mandatory conditions not extractable this run)
tenders.eligibility: Open to all suitably qualified suppliers
tenders.submission_method: Lodge online via buy.nsw / NSW eTendering
tenders.delivery_location: Blacktown Local Government Area, Western Sydney, NSW
tenders.reasoning: Found via search of buy.nsw open tenders (buyer-originated listing on the NSW government procurement portal) and cross-checked against Blacktown City Council references. Closing date/time confirmed across two searches.
tenders.notes: Headline-level only: buy.nsw returned HTTP 403 to the fetch tool this run, so the full detail page, documents, evaluation criteria and contacts could not be scraped. Tender ID not captured this run.

tenders.title: EOI - Construction of Bradfield Bus Interchange (New Bus Service for Western Sydney)
tenders.description: Transport for NSW is seeking Expressions of Interest from pre-qualified contractors to deliver the construction of the Bradfield Bus Interchange as part of the New Bus Service for Western Sydney.
tenders.deliverables_summary: Construction of the Bradfield Bus Interchange - a new bus interchange supporting the New Bus Service for Western Sydney - to be delivered by a pre-qualified contractor. This EOI stage shortlists capable contractors ahead of a subsequent tender/proposal stage.
key_deliverables: Construction of the Bradfield Bus Interchange
Enabling infrastructure for the New Bus Service for Western Sydney
requirements: Pre-qualified contractor status with Transport for NSW (headline eligibility; specific prequalification scheme/level not extractable this run)
tenders.eligibility: Pre-qualified contractors only (Transport for NSW prequalification)
tenders.submission_method: Lodge online via NSW eTendering (Transport for NSW)
tenders.reasoning: Found via search of Transport for NSW open tenders on NSW eTendering (buyer-originated). Cross-checked closing date across searches.
tenders.notes: Headline-level only: NSW eTendering returned HTTP 403 to the fetch tool this run. Tender ID, documents, evaluation criteria and contacts not extractable. EOI (first stage of a multi-stage procurement).

tenders.source_name: buy.nsw / NSW eTendering (Transport for NSW - Roads)
tenders.title: Koala population monitoring (Years 10-15) - Woolgoolga to Ballina Pacific Highway Upgrade
tenders.description: Transport for NSW is seeking experienced ecological specialists to undertake koala population monitoring for years 10-15 in accordance with the Woolgoolga to Ballina Pacific Highway Upgrade Koala Management Plan V6 (Sept 2024).
tenders.deliverables_summary: Provision of ecological specialist services to deliver koala population monitoring across years 10-15 of the Woolgoolga to Ballina Pacific Highway Upgrade, in accordance with the project's Koala Management Plan V6, producing monitoring data and reporting on koala populations in the upgrade corridor.
key_deliverables: Monitoring in accordance with Koala Management Plan V6 (Sept 2024)
requirements: Experienced ecological specialists (headline requirement; specific licences/accreditations not extractable this run)
tenders.eligibility: Open to all suitably experienced ecological specialists
tenders.submission_method: Lodge online via NSW eTendering (Transport for NSW - Roads and Maritime Projects)
tenders.delivery_location: Woolgoolga to Ballina, Pacific Highway corridor, Northern NSW
tenders.reasoning: Found via search of Transport for NSW (Roads and Maritime Projects) open tenders on NSW eTendering (buyer-originated). Scope (koala monitoring years 10-15 per KMP V6) captured from the search snippet.
tenders.notes: Headline-level only: NSW eTendering returned HTTP 403 to the fetch tool this run. Tender ID, documents, evaluation criteria, value and contacts not extractable.

tenders.title: Provision of an Employee Assistance Program (EAP) to service all Local Health Districts
tenders.buyer: Central Coast Local Health District (on behalf of NSW Local Health Districts)
tenders.description: Central Coast Local Health District is requesting quotes for the provision of an Employee Assistance Program (EAP) to service all NSW Local Health Districts.
tenders.deliverables_summary: Provision of an Employee Assistance Program (EAP) - confidential counselling and employee wellbeing/support services - for the staff of all NSW Local Health Districts, procured through Central Coast LHD on behalf of the districts.
key_deliverables: Employee Assistance Program (EAP) counselling and support services
Statewide coverage across all NSW Local Health Districts
Manager support / critical-incident support (typical EAP scope; to be confirmed from documents)
requirements: Suitably qualified EAP provider capable of servicing all NSW Local Health Districts (headline requirement; full mandatory conditions not extractable this run)
tenders.eligibility: Open to all suitably qualified EAP providers
tenders.submission_method: Lodge online via NSW Health eTendering (buy.nsw)
tenders.delivery_location: All NSW Local Health Districts (statewide), NSW
tenders.reasoning: Found via search of NSW Health eTendering opportunities (buyer-originated on the NSW Health procurement portal). Buyer (Central Coast LHD, servicing all LHDs) and closing date captured from search snippet.
tenders.notes: Headline-level only: buy.nsw / NSW Health eTendering returned HTTP 403 to the fetch tool this run. Tender ID, closing time, documents, evaluation criteria and contacts not extractable. Some listed key_deliverables reflect typical EAP scope and need confirmation from the RFQ documents.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Surfaced while searching for Commonwealth 'become a supplier' / GBE procurement portals; a new Australian Government Supplier Portal is launching mid-2026 with supplier registration opening from July 2026 as part of the federal procurement modernisation agenda.
discovered_sources.notes: Buyer-originated Commonwealth registration database (not an aggregator). Currently a capability/registration directory rather than a tender board; worth monitoring as agencies begin sourcing capability through it. Complements AusTender. Origin not fetched this run (Tier-1 gov.au domains WAF-blocked).

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Found while investigating the Commonwealth Games 2026 / precinct construction procurement; Development Victoria (the state's development GBE) publishes EOIs and tenders directly on its own 'Doing business with us' page as well as via Buying for Victoria.
discovered_sources.notes: Buyer-originated Victorian development agency running major works EOIs/RFPs. Not previously in the known-source list. Origin returned HTTP 403 to the fetch tool this run; individual opportunity freshness (e.g. Games-related works) could not be confirmed and was not captured as a tender.

discovered_sources.source_name: SAP Ariba Discovery / SAP Business Network Discovery
discovered_sources.how_discovered: Mandatory Tier 3 check each run; queried for publicly-visible Australian RFx.
discovered_sources.notes: Registration-gated: the opportunity list requires a free SAP Business Network supplier account. No publicly-visible AU RFx was extractable without an authenticated session this run. Recorded as a portal source only - not fabricated as tenders. Largest single slice of corporate opportunity; an authenticated account is recommended for future runs.
